campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investment accounts performed and overall spending. The council also discussed plans for improving how people get around Campbell using different types of transportation like buses, bikes, and walking paths.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment account performance and returns
City budget and spending report for July
New multimodal transportation plan to improve transit options
Workplan timeline for implementing transportation improvements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city rule) and skipped the normal first reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance is now scheduled to be officially adopted at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
First reading waived to expedite the approval process
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting notes from several sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting focused on administrative housekeeping and moving forward with a construction project, though the full details of the construction work aren't completely clear from the available information.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the contract
Closed session discussions (specific topics not detailed)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council reviewed how well the city is serving kids and teens, including youth programs, summer safety initiatives, and job training efforts. They also checked on how code enforcement is working and discussed improvements to library buildings and services.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and summer safety programs for young people
Code enforcement performance and compliance
Library facility upgrades and customer service improvements
Youth intervention and job training services

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included both in-person and virtual participation options. Residents were able to join online via Zoom or by phone to watch and speak during the meeting.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
How to raise hand or unmute to speak during the meeting

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026 at 7 P.M. where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official notes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission meeting and flag ceremony
Personal stories shared by three commissioners
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss three main initiatives: a Budget and Bites presentation (likely about food programs and city spending), a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from March and opened the floor for residents to speak about any topics within the council's authority.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino Summer Jobs program
Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and online options so residents can participate however works best for them. The city is making it easy for people to join from home using Zoom or by phone, and residents can submit written comments before the meeting through the city's website.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and remote via Zoom)
Remote participation options: Zoom link or phone dial-in
Written public comments can be submitted online before the meeting
Meeting accessible through City Council Agenda page on Legistar

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2026, consider changes to how council vacancies are filled, and handle routine meeting business like approving previous minutes and scheduling future meetings. Council members also submitted requests for new items to work on in upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 5-year federal funding plan (2025-2030)
Change to council vacancy rules (specific reduction not detailed in agenda)
New work requests from individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and calendar scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve gas utility rates and financial plans for the next fiscal year. The meeting reviewed previous meeting minutes and discussed adjustments to residential and commercial gas service rates across three service categories.
keyTopics: Gas utility rates increasing for FY 2027
Changes to residential, commercial, and large commercial gas pricing
Transfer of funds between utility reserves and general city fund
Financial forecast approval for gas utility operations
